pageTitle: All cities with siding replacement cost data | Woogoro
metaDescription: Siding replacement cost data by city and state. Vinyl vs fiber-cement, hurricane code, and tear-off load drive the per-square-foot number more than aesthetic choice.
ogTitle: Siding replacement cost by city | Woogoro
ogDescription: Siding pricing by city. Vinyl vs fiber-cement vs engineered wood, hurricane add-ons, and tear-off norms across metros.
schemaDescription: Directory of U.S. cities with 2026 siding replacement cost data, organized by state, including dominant material and tear-off context per metro.
intro: Siding replacement pricing falls into climate-driven materials, not aesthetic ones. Vinyl dominates the Midwest at $4–$7 per square foot installed; fiber-cement (James Hardie, LP SmartSide) takes the Mid-Atlantic and West Coast in the $9–$15 range; engineered wood is gaining on the rainy side of the Pacific Northwest. Hurricane-prone counties (FL, eastern NC, coastal TX) layer impact-rated nailing patterns and wind-rated panels that add $1–$2/sqft over the same product on a Kansas City rambler. Tear-off is routinely 30% of the job when the existing siding hides asbestos backer or pre-2008 vinyl on furring strips that have to come down to inspect sheathing. City-by-city pages cover material mix, climate-driven add-ons, and the tear-off load that historically lands on quotes in that area.
